title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but rather misunderstood. The core issue is not retrieval itself but its narrow definition. RAG should encompass vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters applied iteratively by an agent, with embeddings treated as cached compute based on query volume.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer pushes back on the claim that RAG is dead, arguing the problem is a narrow definition rather than retrieval itself. The talk reframes RAG to span vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters applied iteratively by an agent, instead of treating retrieval as a single vector-similarity lookup. It also positions embeddings as cached compute justified by query volume. The takeaway: retrieval remains central to agentic workflows when defined broadly across multiple search methods.
